solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int hardestWorker(int n, vector<vector<int>>& logs) {
        int maxTime = logs[0][1];
        int resultId = logs[0][0];
        int prevTime = 0;

        for (int i = 1; i < logs.size(); ++i) {
            int id = logs[i][0];
            int currTime = logs[i][1];
            int duration = currTime - prevTime;

            if (duration > maxTime) {
                maxTime = duration;
                resultId = id;
            } else if (duration == maxTime) {
                resultId = min(resultId, id);
            }

            prevTime = currTime;
        }

        return resultId;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(m)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through the logs once, calculating the duration of each task by subtracting the previous task's end time from the current task's end time. It keeps track of the maximum duration and the smallest id in case of ties. The key strengths are its linear time complexity and constant space usage, while the weakness is that it assumes logs are sorted by leaveTime, which is given in the problem constraints.
